description.value: A spiritual imprint left by the Jardúttr — the ancient geothermal spirit guardian of the Echoing Caves. It is not an object but a mark of recognition, carried in the bearer’s awareness. Those who showed mercy to the Jardúttr find that old, deep places seem less hostile; passages reveal themselves; ancient wards hesitate before closing.
description.director:
ACQUISITION: Granted only on the mercy path in Beat 10. If the party defeats but spares the Jardúttr instead of killing it, the Jardúttr acknowledges their restraint and leaves a spiritual imprint on the party.

EFFECT (Passive, permanent for Act 2): The bearer gains an edge on Intuition and Reason tests related to: navigation in cave and underground environments, ancient sacred places and ruins, geothermal or volcanic terrain, and passages used by non-human beings (Vanir, creatures, spirits). This imprint fades when Act 2 concludes.

FICTION: This is not a physical object. The Jardúttr leaves a mark of recognition — a deep-earth attunement that helps those who showed mercy to find their way through old places. Kaelen describes it as “walking with something large just ahead, clearing the path.”
